understanding.goal_understanding: The objective was to maneuver the selectable piece(s) around the grid—potentially by pushing blocks and switching control between multiple selectables—to reach a goal tile (shown as + in the semantic grid), within the available step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Positions were given in pixel coordinates with 3 pixels per grid cell, so each MOVE action attempted to shift the currently selected piece by 3 pixels in the chosen direction. Movement could be stopped by walls/boundaries or other objects; the movement_blocked flags were only hints about adjacent occupancy and did not fully determine success. Pushable objects (blocks) could be pushed if they were directly adjacent in the movement direction and space/interaction rules allowed, causing the block to shift along with the selected piece. Levels could contain multiple selectables: using CLICK with an exact object position swapped which piece was controlled.
